cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The user wants to know how to save money when visiting Disney World according to the passage provided. The options are A through D. First, I'll go back to the passage to find the relevant information. The passage mentions that peak seasons are costly for accommodations. So option A says "By going there in the peak seasons." Wait, that's the opposite. The passage says peak seasons are expensive, so going during peak times would not save money. So A is probably not correct. Next, option B: "By staying outside the resort for a week or two." The passage states, "Staying outside the resort is a choice that may save you a large amount of money if you plan to stay for a week or two." So that directly supports option B. That's a good candidate. Option C: "By buying food ahead of time in Disney World." Wait, the passage says to "buy food ahead of time. If you have most of your meals at your hotel or at cheaper restaurants, it will save you a lot of money. You can try not to buy food in and around Disney World." So buying food ahead of time
